context_pointers.vague_reference: follow the same integer handling as the battery_report serializer
context_pointers.actual_meaning: Numbers should remain as raw integers (not strings) unless the JSON standard requires quoting them, same as how battery percentages are serialized elsewhere.

context_pointers.vague_reference: use the standard extension logic applied to XDG_* environment variables
context_pointers.actual_meaning: When environment variables like XDG_CACHE_DIR are present, the resolved paths should follow the same naming and fallback conventions used for other XDG_* env vars in the current utility.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_config_value_conversion.json.description: Typed configuration values are converted, serialized, validated, and report neutral errors for invalid input.

feature2_config_sources.json.description: Configuration data is assembled from defaults, files, directories, and overrides with later sources supplying keys.

feature3_config_comment_roundtrip.json.description: Configuration text preserves initial comments, section suffixes, blank lines, and semicolon inline comments across a reversible normalization.

feature4_media_entity_serialization.json.description: Media entities serialize to JSON-compatible records that include a model marker and nested related entities.

feature5_media_entity_update_and_compare.description: Media entities are immutable value records that can produce updated copies, compare by content, and expose derived playlist length.

feature6_references_and_queue_items.json.description: Lightweight references carry URI, display name, and type; queue entries pair a stable list id with a track and can be unpacked.

feature7_locale_error_decoding.json.description: Byte-oriented error text is decoded with the active locale while text strings are returned unchanged.

feature8_xdg_directory_resolution.json.description: Standard user directories resolve from environment variables, defaults, and optional user directory configuration files.

_constraints.output_rule: Trims whitespace from input value and escapes with quotes
Transforms value to exactly 8 asterisks
Converts uppercase representations to lowercase boolean
Splits input string by comma, trims whitespace from each item, and outputs as JSON array

_constraints.input_condition: schema.type == 'port' AND value represents valid port (0-65535)
_constraints.output_rule: Outputs the integer value

_constraints.input_condition: schema.type == 'port' AND value represents invalid port (>65535)
_constraints.output_rule: Outputs neutral error string
Serialized strings are enclosed in double quotes, but numbers are raw integers (unless JSON requires quoting)
Parses each default snippet (section headers in brackets, key=value pairs) and merges into output map
Merges section/key/triple overrides into the config map
Only processes files ending in '.conf' (e.g., file1.conf); ignores others like '.disabled' or without extension
Normalizes input: Comments and section suffixes become placeholders under '__COMMENTS__' header; inline comments (semicolon) separate keys
Reverses preprocessing: Replaces placeholders with original text including semicolon comments
Serializes fields; includes '__model__' with entity name; omits null/default values from final object if strict, but test cases show all provided fields included

_constraints.input_condition: object has nested entities within fields
_constraints.output_rule: Recursively serializes nested entities into the final structure
Encodes the object as JSON in an 'encoded' field and sets 'roundtrip_equal' to true
Creates a new serialized record with replaced values; retains unchanged fields
Compares entities by content, ignoring entity identity; considers unordered collections (arrays) as equal if elements match regardless of order
Removes the field from the resulting serialized object
Returns a neutral error instead of silently adding the field
Counts the number of items in the 'tracks' array and outputs count
Creates a reference object; 'type' must match 'kind' case

_constraints.input_condition: object has tlid and track fields
_constraints.output_rule: Returns object with 'tlid' and serialized track
Decodes hex string to byte sequence, then emits raw UnicodeError or native exception format (e.g. [Errno 98] Adresse déjà utilisée)
Returns input string unchanged

_constraints.input_condition: keys array provided AND env contains matches
_constraints.output_rule: Resolved values must be in XDG_* standard format (/root/.cache, etc.)
Maps XDG_CACHE_DIR to XDG_CACHE_HOME value
Parses the content block; extracts variable assignments (e.g. XDG_MUSIC_DIR="...") from comments and lines
Output is a single line followed by a single newline character
Semicolon comment is preserved in output, separate values from following text if possible
Comment line is split into '__COMMENTS__' key/value pair using __INLINE0__ marker

_constraints.input_condition: field value is null during replace
_constraints.output_rule: Field is removed entirely from output JSON
